query: South Korea arms export deals 2026, especially Poland K2 tank second executive contract, any deals expected July-August 2026 worth over $5 billion
answer:
I've completed my research. Here's the key finding for the forecasting system:

**The question's premise contains a timing error.** The Poland K2 tank "second executive contract" was already signed on **August 1, 2025** (worth ~$6.5 billion), not in July-August 2026.

**Bottom line:** No confirmed South Korean arms deal over $5 billion is publicly scheduled to be *signed* specifically in July–August 2026. The relevant July 2026 events are **delivery milestones** (first K9PL howitzers shipped from Changwon July 3, 2026), not new signings. The biggest genuinely *pending* mega-deal is the **UAE package (up to $15 billion)**, which remains at the MoU/negotiation stage following a $35B defense-cooperation MoU signed February 26, 2026.

Full details, data points, and sources are in the structured output above.

sources.title: Poland doubles down on South Korean tanks with $6.5 billion deal
sources.snippet: Poland ordered 180 K2 Black Panther tanks with 81 accompanying vehicles from Hyundai Rotem; the deal worth ~$6.5B was signed Aug 1, 2025 in Gliwice.

sources.title: Hyundai Rotem signs $6.5 billion agreement with Poland for second batch of K2 main battle tanks
sources.snippet: Signing took place Friday Aug 1 at Bumar-Łabędy plant in Gliwice; 116 K2GF delivered 2026-2027, 64 K2PL 2028-2030.

sources.title: South Korea's Hyundai Rotem concludes deliveries of 180 K2 main battle tanks to Poland
sources.snippet: First executive contract signed August 2022 valued at $3.7 billion; second deal signed August 2025.

sources.title: Poland signs agreement to produce South Korean K2 tanks domestically
sources.snippet: Bumar-Łabędy signed an agreement with Hyundai Rotem for domestic K2PL production in April 2026.

sources.title: Hyundai Rotem signs deal to build K2 tanks in Poland
sources.snippet: Hyundai Rotem signed a local production and maintenance cooperation agreement with Bumar-Łabędy for K2PL tanks and armored recovery vehicles, April 2026.

sources.title: First customized K9PL howitzers leave South Korea for Poland under second major artillery contract
sources.snippet: Hanwha shipped first K9PL howitzers from Changwon July 3, 2026, under the second executive contract signed December 1, 2023 (152 vehicles).

sources.title: Hanwha inks $2.6b deal to ship more K9 howitzers to Poland
sources.snippet: Hanwha signed a $2.6 billion contract to ship 152 K9 howitzers to Poland through 2027.

sources.title: South Korea completes delivery of 212 K9 howitzers to Poland under first contract
sources.snippet: All 212 K9A1 howitzers under first contract delivered, final 12 handed over December 16, 2025.

sources.title: South Korea Eyes $15 Billion UAE Arms Deal in Middle East Push
sources.snippet: South Korea is pursuing an arms deal with the UAE worth up to $15 billion.

sources.title: South Korea, UAE Ink $35B Industrial Defense Deal
sources.snippet: South Korea and UAE signed a defense industry cooperation MoU valued at more than $35 billion, part of a broader $65B package (Feb 26, 2026).

sources.title: Korea, UAE agree to pursue $65b in joint projects
sources.snippet: South Korea and UAE agreed to pursue more than $65 billion in joint projects, including over $35B in defense industry cooperation.

sources.title: The Korea Defense Market Is Open
sources.snippet: Korea's defense exports reached $15.4 billion in 2025, surging 60% year-on-year, driven by Poland and NATO-aligned buyers.

sources.title: Poland to Build Tanks Again After 20-Year Break, K2PL Program Could Reach 1,000 Units
sources.snippet: PGZ signed a final agreement with Hyundai Rotem to localize K2PL production; program could reach 1,000 units.

sources.title: Cheongung-II Destroys 29 of 30 Iranian Missiles in UAE Combat Debut
sources.snippet: Cheongung-II achieved ~96% interception rate during Iranian attacks on the UAE in March 2026.

sources.title: Korea Defense Industry 2026: The $37B K-Arms Export Boom
sources.snippet: July 2022 Poland framework worth 17.5 trillion won (~$12B) covered 672 K2 tanks, 648 K9 howitzers, 48 FA-50 jets.

sources.title: Military Weapons in South Korea Trade
sources.snippet: In 2025 South Korea exported $1.02B of military weapons (HS 93.01); top destination Poland ($766M).

key_facts: Poland's K2 tank SECOND executive contract was signed August 1, 2025, worth ~$6.5 billion (some sources $6.7B) — NOT in 2026, contradicting the question's premise.
The second K2 contract covers 180 tanks (116 K2GF for 2026-2027, 64 K2PL for 2028-2030) plus 81 support vehicles, signed at Bumar-Łabędy in Gliwice, Poland.
Poland's first K2 executive contract (Aug 2022, ~$3.4-3.7B) for 180 K2GF tanks was fully delivered by 2025.
In April 2026 (~April 27-28), Bumar-Łabędy/PGZ signed a K2PL localization/production agreement with Hyundai Rotem — an industrial roadmap, not a new procurement contract.
Both K2 executive contracts derive from the July 2022 framework for up to 1,000 K2 tanks; a possible third executive contract for remaining tranches is anticipated but NOT yet signed as of July 2026.
K9 howitzer second executive contract (~$2.6B, 152 units) was signed December 2023; first K9PL howitzers shipped from Changwon on July 3, 2026 (a delivery milestone).
The largest genuinely pending mega-deal is with the UAE, valued up to $15 billion, still at MoU/negotiation stage (a $35B defense-cooperation MoU was signed Feb 26, 2026).
No confirmed South Korean arms deal over $5 billion is publicly scheduled to be signed specifically in July-August 2026.
South Korea's 2025 defense exports reached $15.4 billion, up ~60% year-on-year; Korea ranks ~9th-10th globally in arms exports (~3% market share).
Cheongung-II (KM-SAM Block II) had a combat debut in the UAE in March 2026 with a reported ~96% interception rate against Iranian missiles, boosting Gulf demand.
